EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT FROM MULTIPLE COUNTIES REPORTED 5-10 COAL TRAIN CARS WERE DERAILED BETWEEN ROADS Y AND Z AND 4600 AND 4700 ROADS. BASED ON OTHER REPORTS FROM SPOTTERS AND NUCKOLLS COUNTY DISPATCH, TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FROM APPROXIMATELY 6:26 THROUGH 6:36 PM CDT.
LANDSPOUT TORNADO REPORTED BY MULTIPLE SOURCES IN THE SEDAN AREA. FORMED UNDER A VERY WEAK THUNDERSTORM ALONG A SHARP SURFACE BOUNDARY.
RESIDENCE IS FLOODED AND OCCUPANTS ARE TRAPPED IN THEIR HOME.
WATER RUNNING OVER THE ROAD NEAR THE POND AT KENESAW BLVD AND OVERLAND AVE.

NUMEROUS ROADS ARE IMPASSIBLE IN NORTHWEST ADAMS COUNTY. THIS INCLUDES 94TH ROAD, CONSTITUTION AVE, HOLSTEIN AVE, WANDA AVE. FIRE CHIEF EXPECTS WATER OVER THE ROAD AT OVERLAND AND 42ST SOON. ALONG THE DENMAN HIGHWAY NEAR THE ADAMS/HALL COUNTY LINE THERE WAS WATER AT LEAST 4 INCHES DEEP FOR A 1/2 MILE AND RUNNING OVER THE ROAD.
ROADS CREWS AND LAW ENFORCEMENT CANNOT EVEN GET TO THE ROADS TO ASSESS THE DAMAGE. ROADS IMPASSIBLE IN SOUTHWEST HALL COUNTY.
